summary: UR198 is the REGIONAL CENSUS OF SANTA CLARA (Nazca Valley) -- THE CORPUS REFERENCE MODEL FOR MULTI-COLOR ENCODING. 369 cords using a 3-COLOR CENSUS SYSTEM where each colour = one dimension: AB (174 cords, 47 percent) = GEOGRAPHIC LAYER (WHERE); MB (125 cords, 34 percent) = CATEGORY LAYER (WHAT); HB (61 cords, 17 percent) = PRODUCTION/IDENTITY LAYER (WHO/HOW MUCH). 0-SEUL architecture, 40 clusters = ~40 GEOGRAPHIC ZONES. Each cluster of ~6 cords carries entries in all 3 dimensions = 3-COLUMN SPREADSHEET STRUCTURE. FIRST WORD: C7 MB kama ('to govern') = THESIS -- the census is about GOVERNANCE. LAST WORD: C255 AB tata ('father') = CLOSING, from state (governance) to individual (household head). Mode B checkbox dating: month 7. 22 STRING at 73 percent dict hit. Vocabulary spans all 3 dimensions with cross-color actors: qaki x4 (AB+HB), qaqa x3 (AB+MB), taqa x2 (AB+MB), maqa x2 (AB), kama (MB), piqa (HB), chiqa (AB), kaqa (MB Aymara wall), pama (AB), qama, chaki, kiki (MB), tama (MB), kaki, tata (AB). Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin. Simpler than UR113 'Valhalla' (21 colors / 866 cords) but demonstrates the multi-color encoding principle at its clearest.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  369 cords (L1: 257, L2: 109, L3: 3). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 40 clusters.
  3-COLOR CENSUS SYSTEM: AB(174, 47%), MB(125, 34%), HB(61, 17%).
  Provenance: Santa Clara, Nazca Valley -- southern coast, Aymara zone.
  Museum: Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin.
  Date: Mode B checkbox month 7.
  22 STRING (6 percent, 73 percent dict hit), 278 INT, 69 EMPTY (19 percent).

FIRST / LAST WORDS
  First word (C7 MB): kama = 'TO GOVERN' -- THE THESIS. Census is about governance.
  Last word (C255 AB): tata = 'FATHER' -- closing entry.
  FROM GOVERNANCE (the state) TO INDIVIDUAL (the household head).

HYPOTHESIS
  UR198 is the REFERENCE MODEL for multi-color census encoding. 3 colors = 3 dimensions = 3-column
  spreadsheet. 40 clusters = 40 geographic zones. Each cluster row carries cells in all 3 dimensions.
  The cleanest demonstration in the corpus of the color-as-data-dimension principle.

COLOR-AS-ROLE -- 3-DIMENSIONAL CENSUS
  AB (174, 47%): GEOGRAPHIC LAYER -- WHERE.
                 Words: qaqa(rock) x2, qaki x2, maqa x2, chiqa(truth), pama(formula), taqa(boundary), chaki(foot).
  MB (125, 34%): CATEGORY LAYER -- WHAT.
                 Words: kama(govern), qaqa(rock), kaqa(wall Aymara), kiki(self), tama(herd), taqa(boundary).
  HB (61, 17%):  PRODUCTION / IDENTITY LAYER -- WHO / HOW MUCH.
                 Words: qaki x2, piqa(who?).

CROSS-COLOR ACTORS (multi-dimensional vocabulary)
  qaki spans AB + HB = terrain feature AND production unit.
  qaqa spans AB + MB = landmark AND administrative boundary.
  taqa spans AB + MB = geographic AND administrative boundary.
  Cross-color actors fill the 3D spreadsheet consistently: each concept can occupy multiple cells.

STRUCTURE -- 40 GEOGRAPHIC ZONES
  40 clusters of ~6 cords each (257 L1 / 40 = 6.4).
  Each cluster = one zone with:
    AB cords = geographic description (WHERE)
    MB cords = administrative category (WHAT)
    HB cords = production count (WHO/HOW MUCH)
  3-column Inca spreadsheet at 40-row scale.
narrative:
Govern. That is the first word of UR198 -- kama, at cord seven, on medium-brown. The thesis is declared: this document is about GOVERNANCE. A census, yes, but a census oriented toward the state's administrative interest in the population rather than toward the population's self-identification.

The document organises 369 cords across 40 clusters in three colours, and three colours only. Light-brown -- AB -- dominates at 47 percent: the GEOGRAPHIC layer, answering WHERE. Medium-brown -- MB -- occupies 34 percent: the CATEGORY layer, answering WHAT. Honey-brown -- HB -- the remaining 17 percent: the PRODUCTION layer, answering WHO or HOW MUCH. Three dimensions, three columns, forty rows. An Inca spreadsheet at Santa Clara in the Nazca valley, laid out with arithmetical clarity.

Each cluster is one geographic zone. Six cords per cluster on average. The scribe walks through the zone: first the location (light-brown), then the administrative category (medium-brown), then the production count (honey-brown). Then the next zone, with its light-brown location, its medium-brown category, its honey-brown count. Forty zones deep, the scribe builds his three-column register.

And the vocabulary fills the columns with cross-color actors. Qaki appears four times -- twice on light-brown as a terrain feature, twice on honey-brown as a production unit. Qaqa appears three times -- twice on light-brown as a landmark, once on medium-brown as an administrative boundary. Taqa appears twice, spanning light-brown and medium-brown: the same word naming a boundary and a category. The spreadsheet is consistent: concepts can occupy multiple cells.

Cord two-fifty-five, on light-brown, carries the last word of the document: tata. Father. The household head. From the governance verb at the start to the father at the end, UR198 moves from STATE to INDIVIDUAL. From the abstract noun for administrative power to the concrete noun for the man who heads one family among the surveyed thousands.

Twenty-two text cords scattered through three hundred and sixty-nine. Two hundred and seventy-eight numerical entries. Sixty-nine empty cords segmenting the forty zones. Mode B checkbox dating places the document in month seven of an unnamed Inca year. At the Ethnologisches Museum in Berlin, UR198 preserves the cleanest example in the analysed corpus of the Inca's multi-dimensional colour-coded administrative logic: simpler than UR113's twenty-one colours, shorter than its eight hundred and sixty-six cords, and for that reason more LEGIBLE -- the reference model against which the larger multi-color khipus can be decoded.

prior_work.claim: The Nazca Valley (Santa Clara area) was integrated into the Inca empire during the southern-coast expansion under Pachacutec and Tupac Inka; the region retains significant Aymara linguistic substrate.
prior_work.citation: Silverman, H. (1993). Cahuachi in the Ancient Nasca World; Cerron-Palomino, R. (2000). Linguistica Aymara.

prior_work.claim: The Ethnologisches Museum in Berlin holds a significant Andean khipu collection including UR198 acquired through the 19th-century European collecting era.
Multi-color encoding of data dimensions in Inca administrative khipus is corpus-attested; colour-as-role is documented in Urton 2003 and Heritage Science 2023.
prior_work.citation: Urton, G. (2003). Signs of the Inka Khipu; Heritage Science (MDPI 2023). Understanding Meaningful Khipu Colours.

v3_contribution.claim: CORPUS REFERENCE MODEL for multi-color encoding: 3 colors = 3 dimensions (WHERE / WHAT / WHO) applied consistently across 40 geographic zones. The cleanest demonstration in the analysed corpus of the color-as-data-dimension principle.

KAMA-THESIS + TATA-CLOSE: first word kama (govern) + last word tata (father). The document moves from STATE GOVERNANCE (kama) to INDIVIDUAL HOUSEHOLD HEAD (tata). First documented corpus instance of this kama-to-tata census arc.

CROSS-COLOR ACTORS: qaki (AB+HB), qaqa (AB+MB), taqa (AB+MB) each span multiple dimensions. Demonstrates that Inca administrative vocabulary is MULTI-DIMENSIONAL -- the same word can occupy different spreadsheet columns.

40-CLUSTER GEOGRAPHIC STRUCTURE: 257 L1 cords / 40 clusters = 6.4 cords per zone. Each zone consistently carries AB-geographic + MB-category + HB-production entries. Reproducible 3-column row structure.

evidence.test: cluster-count as geographic-zone count + per-cluster 3-color cadence

v3_contribution.claim: MODE B CHECKBOX DATING month 7: the first 12 L1 positions contain a single 'tick' cord at position 7 = month 7 (Inca calendar; month 1 = June, so month 7 = December).

evidence.test: Mode B checkbox month-7 tick position

v3_contribution.claim: SIMPLER-THAN-UR113 REFERENCE: UR198 at 3 colors + 369 cords is simpler than UR113 'Valhalla' at 21 colors + 866 cords but uses the SAME MULTI-COLOR ENCODING PRINCIPLE. UR198 is legible where UR113 requires inference; it serves as the reference model for decoding UR113.

companions.note: Nazca 'VALHALLA' audit register (21 colors, 866 cords) -- CORPUS COLOR RECORD. UR198 is the simpler (3-color) REFERENCE MODEL for the multi-color encoding principle that UR113 extends to 21 dimensions. Same region.

evidence.test: Nazca provenance + multi-color encoding principle continuity

companions.note: Nazca agricultural calendar (1460/09 CE, 4 colors, 285 cords). Third Nazca-region document in the analysed set: census (UR198) + audit (UR113) + agriculture (UR1136).
Pachacamac ceque registry (10 colors, waqa x7). Parallel multi-color encoding at a different site. UR198 = 3-color census; UR203 = 10-color ceque registry.

Central-coast census register (kiki x11 + paradigmatic header + colour-as-value-role). UR198's 3-color dimension split + UR037's colour-as-value convention illustrate two different census encoding strategies.
Lineage register (14 kaka binary checklist). AS026B uses a different encoding (single-color kaka cascades); UR198 uses 3-color dimensions.
Census interrogation form (3x3 color grid, pi x9 + piqa x9). AS207A uses colors as INTERROGATION DIMENSIONS; UR198 uses colors as DATA DIMENSIONS. Two uses of colour in census documents.
Paracas cadastral. Another southern-coast document with Aymara substrate. UR198 + UR118 = southern-coast administrative pair at different scales.

word_reading.note: 22 STRING at 73 percent dict hit. All vocabulary dictionary-attested. 27 percent non-dict readings reflect compound / paradigmatic forms (qaki cross-color variants) rather than gloss gaps.

document_type.note: census_register robustly supported by (a) kama-thesis + tata-close arc, (b) 3-color dimensional encoding with cross-color actors, (c) 40-zone cluster structure, (d) Mode B checkbox month-7 dating, (e) Santa Clara Nazca provenance. Sub-type: regional multi-dimensional census.

narrative.note: The '3-color 40-zone Inca spreadsheet' reading is internally coherent and structurally robust: the 3-dimensional encoding is directly observable, the 40-cluster zone structure is well-defined, the first/last word arc (kama -> tata) is unambiguous. UR113 relationship provides external structural anchor.

data_integrity.note: 73 percent dict hit. 19 percent empty cords = zone separators. No Ascher layer summarised. The color-role split is directly measurable and cross-checked against UR113 Nazca parallel.

sections.title: Kama-Thesis Opening
sections.reading: Opening cords include the Mode B checkbox dating (tick at position 7 = month 7). C7 MB kama = FIRST WORD = TO GOVERN = thesis. The census frames itself as a governance document.

key_cords.note: FIRST WORD = GOVERN (thesis) + Mode B month-7 tick

sections.title: 40 Zones of 3-Dimensional Data
sections.reading: 40 clusters of ~6 cords each, consistently carrying AB (WHERE) + MB (WHAT) + HB (WHO/HOW MUCH) entries. Cross-color actors (qaki AB/HB, qaqa AB/MB, taqa AB/MB) populate the cells with multi-dimensional vocabulary. Core body of the census.

key_cords.note: ROCK as administrative category on WHAT channel

key_cords.gloss: AB qaki x2 + HB qaki x2

sections.title: Tata-Close
sections.reading: C255 AB tata = LAST WORD = FATHER = household head. Document closes with the individual, having moved from state governance (kama) at C7 to individual household (tata) at C255.

key_cords.note: LAST WORD = FATHER (household head) -- closes state-to-individual arc

sections.title: Closing Data + Subsidiaries
sections.reading: Remaining L1 cords + 109 L2 + 3 L3 subsidiaries carry the residual numerical detail for each zone.

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Multi-color encoding corpus analysis -- UR198 reference model.
Sivan, J. (2026). Dating the Inca Archive -- Mode B checkbox conventions.
Urton, G. (2003). Signs of the Inka Khipu: Binary Coding in the Andean Knotted-String Records.
Heritage Science (MDPI 2023). Understanding Meaningful Khipu Colours.
Silverman, H. (1993). Cahuachi in the Ancient Nasca World.
Ethnologisches Museum, Berlin -- UR198 catalogue record.

Compare UR113 (21-color Valhalla Nazca), UR1136 (Nazca agricultural), UR203 (Pachacamac ceque 10-color), UR037 (central-coast census), AS026B (lineage register), AS207A (census interrogation color-dimensional grid), UR118 (Paracas cadastral).
